prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (chemkit tier `standard`) in the gas phase. The calculation was for the neutral molecule with a charge of 0 and a spin multiplicity of 1.

The calculation successfully converged, and no warnings were reported by the engine.

The results predict the sites for electrophilic and nucleophilic attack:
*   **Most electrophilic site (most susceptible to nucleophilic attack):** Atom C4, with the largest Fukui(+) index of 0.49642.
*   **Most nucleophilic site (most susceptible to electrophilic attack):** Atom C5, with the largest Fukui(-) index of 0.10729.

The full Fukui indices for all atoms are provided below. `f+` predicts reactivity towards a nucleophile, `f-` predicts reactivity towards an electrophile, and `f0` predicts reactivity towards a radical.

| Atom Index | Atom | f(+) | f(-) | f(0) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | O | 0.47317 | 0.09702 | 0.28509 |
| 2 | O | 0.47344 | 0.09677 | 0.28511 |
| 3 | N | -0.45670 | 0.01724 | -0.21973 |
| 4 | C | 0.49642 | -0.01429 | 0.24106 |
| 5 | C | 0.11563 | 0.10729 | 0.11146 |
| 6 | C | 0.11588 | 0.10724 | 0.11156 |
| 7 | C | 0.17412 | 0.09492 | 0.13452 |
| 8 | C | 0.17423 | 0.09489 | 0.13456 |
| 9 | C | 0.28914 | 0.00831 | 0.14872 |
| 10 | H | -0.23169 | 0.07521 | -0.07824 |
| 11 | H | -0.23182 | 0.07520 | -0.07831 |
| 12 | H | -0.13372 | 0.08388 | -0.02492 |
| 13 | H | -0.13379 | 0.08388 | -0.02496 |
| 14 | H | -0.12431 | 0.07243 | -0.02594 |
